framework: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (NIST AI 100-1)

criteria: domain_mismatch: NLI contradiction >80% on Equal/Superset (critical) or >50% (moderate)
vocabulary_inflation: bi-encoder/STS gap >0.15 with STS <0.60 (moderate) or >0.12 (low)

critical_elements.title: Continual improvement integration
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Data Governance model lifecycle KSA but outcome is about integrating measurable continual improvement activities into AI system updates with stakeholder engagement. Functional domain should be RM or AG, not DG.

critical_elements.title: High-priority risk response development
critical_elements.issue: Matched to EU AI Act risk classification task but outcome is about general AI risk response strategy (mitigate, transfer, avoid, accept). RC-T-012 is regulation-specific; this outcome needs a broader RM-domain KSA for AI risk response planning.

critical_elements.title: Risk tracking infrastructure
critical_elements.issue: Classified Equal at STS 0.689 but NLI contradiction 98.1%. Outcome is about establishing risk tracking infrastructure for emergent/unanticipated risks. Matched to AG impact assessment skill — overlapping but functionally different. May need RM-domain KSA for ongoing risk tracking.

critical_elements.title: Measurement result validation and communication
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Leadership diagnostic assessment skill but outcome is about validating AI trustworthiness measurements with domain expert input. Functional domain should be RM or MS (measurement), not LS.

critical_elements.title: Adjudicated feedback incorporation
critical_elements.issue: Classified Equal at STS 0.717 but NLI contradiction 97.8%. AG governance review process skill shares vocabulary but outcome specifically requires feedback adjudication and systematic incorporation into AI design — a distinct competency.

critical_elements.title: Third-party AI contingency processes
critical_elements.issue: Matched to vendor partnership negotiation skill but outcome is about contingency planning for third-party AI system/data failures. Functional domain should be RM or SP (supply chain risk), not LS.

critical_elements.title: AI testing, incident identification, and information sharing
critical_elements.issue: Matched to responsible AI principles knowledge but outcome is about establishing AI testing infrastructure, incident identification mechanisms, and information sharing practices. Functional domain is AI/SP (testing, incident response), not AG.

analysis.summary: 14 of 70 elements (20.0%) flagged with gap signals. 7 critical, 3 moderate, 4 low. Gap rate is lower than STRM-003-DCWF (22.9%) and STRM-002-NICE (24.0%) but higher than STRM-001-ONET (4.8%) and STRM-004-DDAT (40.2%).

pattern_analysis.ag_gravity_well_continues: AG-domain KSAs appear in 3 of 7 critical gaps (AIRMF-MS-3.1, AIRMF-MG-2.4, AIRMF-GV-4.3). This is consistent with the AG gravity well pattern identified in STRM-005-EU-AI-ACT — governance vocabulary overlaps with risk management outcome language, pulling bi-encoder candidate selection toward AG KSAs.
pattern_analysis.cross_domain_matching: Critical gaps span 4 matched domains (DG, RC, AG, LS) across 3 NIST functions (GOVERN, MEASURE, MANAGE). The MAP function produced zero critical gaps, suggesting strong WIDAI coverage of context-setting and categorization competencies.
pattern_analysis.outcome_vs_competency_gap: Several critical gaps (MG-4.2, MS-3.1, MS-4.1) involve NIST AI RMF outcomes that describe organizational capabilities (risk tracking, measurement validation, continual improvement) where the best WIDAI match is a related but functionally different individual competency. This suggests potential need for organizational-capability KSAs in the RM domain.

strongest_domain_coverage.AG: AI Governance — strongest coverage for GOVERN function outcomes. Legitimate matches for policy integration, human oversight, and risk culture.
strongest_domain_coverage.RM: Risk Management — strong coverage for risk assessment, risk tolerance, and risk response outcomes.
strongest_domain_coverage.AI: AI Systems — good coverage for TEVV, model documentation, and AI technical outcomes.

gap_themes.theme: AI Risk Tracking and Emergent Risk Monitoring
gap_themes.description: Establishing infrastructure for tracking existing, unanticipated, and emergent AI risks over time. Distinct from one-time risk assessment — requires ongoing monitoring capability.
Validating AI trustworthiness measurements with domain expert input and communicating results to stakeholders. Measurement governance competency.
Developing risk response strategies (mitigate, transfer, avoid, accept) for high-priority AI risks. Currently matched to EU-specific RC task; needs framework-agnostic RM KSA.
Contingency planning for third-party AI system and data failures. Supply chain risk management for AI dependencies.
Establishing organizational practices for AI testing, incident identification, and information sharing. Infrastructure competency, not just governance knowledge.
Integrating measurable improvement activities into AI system update cycles with stakeholder engagement.

analysis.cross_strm_note: Several gap themes corroborate findings from previous STRMs: AI incident response/testing infrastructure (STRM-005 EUAIA-O-047, STRM-002 NICE-GAP-002), third-party AI risk management (STRM-002 NICE-GAP-005, STRM-003 DCWF-GAP-003). The measurement validation and risk response strategy themes are novel to STRM-006. Cross-STRM corroboration strengthens the case for RM-domain KSA creation during Phase 1D synthesis.
